reviews.comment:
It is funny that they said they don’t have a physical food menu and wanted me to wait at the counter and see the display screen changing and then order food. Surprisingly they wanted me to order and then take a seat. Why do you not have a food menu where customer can sit and decide what to order.

Unfortunately I didn’t get to experience their food but never mind I will visit again.

reviewReply.comment:
This feedback is noted. The ordering flow should feel intuitive, not confusing, and we’re reviewing how this is communicated on the floor.
We appreciate you giving us another visit and would love to hear more. You can connect with us at @coffee.island.in or hello@coffeeisland.in.

reviews.name: accounts/100486096577551952970/locations/6119222168702984294/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2pKaU1FSmlWSGxzWDJwM2RXNU5iamM1WXpVNVRrRRAB

reviews.comment:
I ordered The Ethopian global blend beans - I asked the cafe to grind it for Pour over and they did. Thank you very much for that.

But, the ground is way fine. It's for espresso and not pour over.
This ground doesn't seem fresh at all.
There's no roast date & neither did the cafe worker knew the roast date. Sadly meaning the beans were not fresh.
I expected it to come in a pack as seen on the photo on Zomato/Swiggy.
But instead they gave me in a glass.
I made a pour over and unfortunately there was no dry or wet aroma i could appreciate and the coffee seems very average.

I'll surely try and visit the cafe and try their manual brew.
If I find it fresh and like it, I'll surely change my review.

reviewReply.comment:
Thank you for taking the time to share such a detailed note. We’ve carefully read this and flagged the concerns around grind size, roast information, and packaging clarity for review with the team.
We appreciate you giving our manual brews a chance and would be glad to continue the conversation. Please reach out at @coffee.island.in or hello@coffeeisland.in.
